item.title: Routine Access Is Powering Modern Intrusions, a New Threat Report Finds
item.dc:creator: Sponsored by Blackpoint Cyber
item.description: Modern intrusions increasingly start with valid credentials and routine access, not exploits. Blackpoint Cyber's upcoming threat report shows how VPN abuse, RMM tools, and social engineering drive most incidents. [...]

item.title: FBI warns against using Chinese mobile apps due to privacy risks
item.dc:creator: Sergiu Gatlan
item.description: The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) warned Americans against using foreign-developed mobile applications, particularly those created by Chinese developers. [...]

item.title: Google fixes fourth Chrome zero-day exploited in attacks in 2026
item.dc:creator: Sergiu Gatlan
item.description: Google has fixed the fourth Chrome vulnerability exploited in zero-day attacks since the start of the year. [...]

item.title: Google Drive ransomware detection now on by default for paying users
item.dc:creator: Sergiu Gatlan
item.description: Google announced that the AI-powered Google Drive ransomware detection feature has reached general availability and is now enabled by default for all paying users. [...]

item.title: New Windows 11 emergency update fixes preview update install issues
item.dc:creator: Sergiu Gatlan
item.description: Microsoft released an emergency update to fix the March 2026 KB5079391 non-security preview update, which was pulled over the weekend due to installation issues. [...]

item.title: Claude Code source code accidentally leaked in NPM package
item.dc:creator: Mayank Parmar
item.description: Anthropic says it accidentally leaked the source code for Claude Code, which is closed source, but the company says no customer data or credentials were exposed. [...]

item.title: Google now allows you to change your @gmail.com address
item.dc:creator: Mayank Parmar
item.description: Google is rolling out a new feature in the U.S. that allows users to change their @gmail address or create a new alias. [...]

item.title: Proton launches new "Meet" privacy-focused conferencing platform
item.dc:creator: Bill Toulas
item.description: Proton has announced a new video conferencing service named Meet and positioned it as a privacy-focused alternative to mainstream services like Google Meet, Zoom, and Microsoft Teams. [...]

item.title: GIGABYTE Control Center vulnerable to arbitrary file write flaw
item.dc:creator: Bill Toulas
item.description: The GIGABYTE Control Center is vulnerable to an arbitrary file-write flaw that could allow a remote, unauthenticated attacker to access files on vulnerable hosts. [...]

item.title: Claude AI finds Vim, Emacs RCE bugs that trigger on file open
item.dc:creator: Bill Toulas
item.description: Vulnerabilities in the Vim and GNU Emacs text editors, discovered using simple prompts with the Claude assistant, allow remote code execution simply by opening a file. [...]

item.title: Cisco source code stolen in Trivy-linked dev environment breach
item.dc:creator: Lawrence Abrams
item.description: Cisco has suffered a cyberattack after threat actors used stolen credentials from the recent Trivy supply chain attack to breach its internal development environment and steal source code belonging to the company and its customers. [...]

item.title: How to Categorize AI Agents and Prioritize Risk
item.dc:creator: Sponsored by Token Security
item.description: AI agent risk isn't equal, it scales with access to systems and level of autonomy. Token Security explains how CISOs should categorize agents and prioritize what to secure first. [...]

item.title: Hackers compromise Axios npm package to drop cross-platform malware
item.dc:creator: Bill Toulas
item.description: Hackers hijacked the npm account of the Axios package, a JavaScript HTTP client with 100M+ weekly downloads, to deliver remote access trojans to Linux, Windows, and macOS systems. [...]

item.title: Microsoft fixes Outlook Classic crashes caused by Teams Meeting add-in
item.dc:creator: Sergiu Gatlan
item.description: ​Microsoft has resolved a known issue that rendered the classic Outlook email client unusable for users who enabled the Microsoft Teams Meeting Add-in. [...]

item.title: Hacker charged with stealing $53 million from Uranium crypto exchange
item.dc:creator: Sergiu Gatlan
item.description: U.S. prosecutors have charged a Maryland man with stealing more than $53 million after hacking the Uranium Finance crypto exchange twice and laundering the proceeds through a cryptocurrency mixer. [...]
